| Cloud | AWS | Most mature, excellent services |

| Orchestration | ECS (→ EKS) | Managed, simpler than K8s initially |

   - Database encryption at rest (AWS RDS)
   - HTTPS/WSS everywhere

5. **Error Handling** (Week 3)

**Project:** task-automation-hub
**Squad:** Automação Profissional
**Architect:** @architect (Aria)
**Date:** 2026-02-14
**Status:** COMPLETE
**Version:** 1.0

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This document outlines the complete system architecture for task-automation-hub, a full-stack automation platform designed to reduce manual task management by 87-90% while maintaining enterprise-grade security and scalability.

### Key Architectural Decisions

✅ **Approach:** Microservices with API Gateway (future-proof, scalable)
✅ **Frontend:** React 18 + TypeScript + Redux Toolkit (performant, maintainable)
✅ **Backend:** Node.js/Express + WebSockets (real-time, fast iteration)
✅ **Database:** PostgreSQL (primary) + Redis (caching/real-time)
✅ **Infrastructure:** AWS (EC2/ECS, RDS, ElastiCache, CloudFront)
✅ **Security:** OAuth 2.0 + JWT + TLS 1.2+ (enterprise-ready)
✅ **Deployment:** Docker + Kubernetes (scalable, portable)

### 1.2 High-Level Data Flow

```
USER CREATES TASK
    ↓
[1. FRONTEND]
├─ User fills form (title, description, assignee)
├─ Client-side validation
└─ Submits via HTTPS to API
    ↓
[2. API GATEWAY]
├─ Receives request
├─ Validates authentication (JWT)
├─ Routes to Task Service
└─ Rate limiting check
    ↓
[3. TASK SERVICE]
├─ Validate input (FR-002)
├─ Check for duplicates
├─ Auto-assign with intelligence (FR-006)
├─ Save to PostgreSQL
├─ Invalidate Redis cache
└─ Emit event to Message Queue
    ↓
[4. NOTIFICATION SERVICE] (async via RabbitMQ)
├─ Receive task.created event
├─ Send email notification (FR-011)
├─ Send Slack notification (FR-022)
├─ Send in-app notification (WebSocket)
└─ Log notification sent
    ↓
[5. ANALYTICS SERVICE] (async via RabbitMQ)
├─ Record task metrics
├─ Update user's task count
└─ Update dashboard counters
    ↓
[6. FRONTEND (WebSocket)]
├─ Real-time update via WebSocket
├─ New task appears in dashboard
└─ Notification toast shows
    ↓
USER SEES NEW TASK IN REAL-TIME ✓
```

```yaml
Primary Database:
  choice: PostgreSQL 15+
  reason: |
    ✓ ACID compliance (data integrity)
    ✓ Excellent JSON support (documents + relational)
    ✓ Full-text search
    ✓ Window functions for analytics
    ✓ Row-Level Security (RLS)
    ✓ Mature, stable, proven at scale
  alternatives_considered:
    - MySQL: Less features, lower reliability
    - MongoDB: No ACID, eventual consistency risks
    - DynamoDB: Vendor lock-in, high costs at scale

Caching Layer:
  choice: Redis
  reason: |
    ✓ Sub-millisecond latency
    ✓ Perfect for session management
    ✓ Real-time features (pub/sub)
    ✓ Distributed caching
    ✓ Job queue support (Bull)
  alternatives_considered:
    - Memcached: No persistence, no features
    - Elasticsearch: Overkill, for search only

Search:
  choice: PostgreSQL Full-Text Search (optional Elasticsearch later)
  reason: |
    ✓ Built-in, no new infrastructure
    ✓ Sufficient for MVP
    ✓ Upgrade path to Elasticsearch if needed
  alternatives_considered:
    - Elasticsearch: Overkill initially, plan for later
    - Algolia: Cloud service, vendor lock-in

Backup & Replication:
  choice: PostgreSQL Multi-AZ with automated backups
  reason: |
    ✓ Automatic failover
    ✓ Point-in-time recovery
    ✓ Synchronous replication
    ✓ No data loss on failure
```

### 2.4 Infrastructure Stack

**Technology Selection: AWS + Docker + Kubernetes**

```yaml
Cloud Provider:
  choice: AWS
  reason: |
    ✓ Most mature cloud provider
    ✓ Excellent services portfolio
    ✓ RDS for PostgreSQL (managed)
    ✓ ElastiCache for Redis
    ✓ ECS/EKS for containers
    ✓ CloudFront for CDN
  alternatives_considered:
    - GCP: Good, but less enterprise adoption
    - Azure: Good, but worse pricing
    - DigitalOcean: Limited for our scale

Containerization:
  choice: Docker
  reason: |
    ✓ Industry standard
    ✓ Dev/prod parity
    ✓ Easy deployment
    ✓ Works with Kubernetes
  alternatives_considered:
    - Virtual Machines: Slower, heavier

Orchestration:
  choice: AWS ECS (initially), Kubernetes (future)
  reason: |
    ✓ ECS is easier, faster to implement
    ✓ Upgrade path to EKS when needed
    ✓ Multi-region deployment support
    ✓ Auto-scaling built-in
  alternatives_considered:
    - Traditional EC2: No scaling
    - Heroku: Vendor lock-in, expensive

Database Hosting:
  choice: AWS RDS (PostgreSQL Multi-AZ)
  reason: |
    ✓ Automated backups
    ✓ Automatic failover
    ✓ Zero-downtime patching
    ✓ Enhanced monitoring
  alternatives_considered:
    - Self-managed: Too much operational burden
    - DynamoDB: Wrong data model

Caching Hosting:
  choice: AWS ElastiCache (Redis Multi-AZ)
  reason: |
    ✓ Managed Redis
    ✓ Automatic failover
    ✓ Replication
    ✓ No operational burden
  alternatives_considered:
    - Self-managed: Complex, risky

CDN:
  choice: CloudFront + S3
  reason: |
    ✓ Global content delivery
    ✓ Integrated with AWS
    ✓ Excellent performance
    ✓ Cost-effective
  alternatives_considered:
    - Cloudflare: Good, but different ecosystem
    - Fastly: Great, but pricey

Monitoring & Logging:
  choice: CloudWatch + optional Datadog
  reason: |
    ✓ Built into AWS
    ✓ No separate infrastructure
    ✓ Good enough for MVP
    ✓ Upgrade to Datadog if needed
  alternatives_considered:
    - Splunk: Enterprise, complex
    - ELK Stack: Self-managed, complex
```

input.content:
version: '3.8'

services:
  # PostgreSQL Database
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    container_name: task-hub-postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: dev_user
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: dev_password
      POSTGRES_DB: task_automation_hub
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U dev_user"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - task-hub-network

  # Redis Cache
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: task-hub-redis
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - task-hub-network

  # pgAdmin (PostgreSQL Management UI)
  pgadmin:
    image: dpage/pgadmin4:latest
    container_name: task-hub-pgadmin
    environment:
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: admin@example.com
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: admin
    ports:
      - "5050:80"
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    networks:
      - task-hub-network

  # Backend Service (optional for local dev)
  # Uncomment to run backend in container instead of local
  # backend:
  #   build:
  #     context: ./backend
  #     dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
  #   container_name: task-hub-backend

version: '3.8'

services:
  # PostgreSQL Database
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    container_name: task-hub-postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: dev_user
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: dev_password
      POSTGRES_DB: task_automation_hub
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U dev_user"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - task-hub-network

  # Redis Cache
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: task-hub-redis
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - task-hub-network

  # pgAdmin (PostgreSQL Management UI)
  pgadmin:
    image: dpage/pgadmin4:latest
    container_name: task-hub-pgadmin
    environment:
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: admin@example.com
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: admin
    ports:
      - "5050:80"
    depends_on:

input.content:
# 🚀 Task Automation Hub

**Status:** Phase 3 Implementation (Week 1 Setup)

A full-stack task automation platform reducing manual task management by 87-90% while maintaining enterprise-grade security and scalability.

---

## 📋 Quick Start

### Prerequisites
- Node.js 18+ (LTS)
- Docker & Docker Compose
- Git

### Local Development Setup

```bash
# 1. Clone and navigate
git clone <repo-url>
cd task-automation-hub

# 2. Start Docker services (PostgreSQL + Redis)
docker-compose up -d

# 3. Setup Backend
cd backend
npm install
npm run db:migrate          # Run Prisma migrations
npm run db:seed             # Seed test data
npm run dev                 # Start dev server on port 3001

# 4. In new terminal, setup Frontend
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev                 # Start Vite dev server on port 5173
```

### Verify Services
- **Frontend:** http://localhost:5173
- **Backend API:** http://localhost:3001
- **pgAdmin:** http://localhost:5050 (admin@example.com / admin)
- **Redis:** localhost:6379

---

## 🏗️ Project Structure

```
task-automation-hub/
├── backend/                 # Express.js + Prisma
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── index.ts        # App entry point
│   │   ├── routes/         # API routes
│   │   ├── services/       # Business logic
│   │   ├── controllers/    # Request handlers
│   │   ├── middleware/     # Auth, validation, error handling
│   │   ├── models/         # Data models (Prisma)
│   │   ├── types/          # TypeScript types
│   │   ├── config/         # Configuration
│   │   └── websocket/      # Socket.IO setup
│   ├── prisma/
│   │   ├── schema.prisma   # Database schema
│   │   └── migrations/     # DB migrations
│   ├── tests/              # Jest test files
│   └── package.json
│
├── frontend/                # React 18 + Vite
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.tsx        # React entry point
│   │   ├── App.tsx         # Root component
│   │   ├── features/       # Feature modules
│   │   │   ├── auth/       # Login/Register
│   │   │   ├── tasks/      # Task CRUD
│   │   │   └── notifications/
│   │   ├── components/     # Shared components
│   │   ├── hooks/          # Custom React hooks
│   │   ├── store/          # Redux state management
│   │   ├── services/       # API client services
│   │   ├── types/          # TypeScript types
│   │   └── styles/         # Global styles
│   ├── vite.config.ts
│   ├── tsconfig.json
│   └── package.json
│
├── docs/
│   ├── phase-1-analysis/
│   ├── phase-2-design/
│   └── phase-3-implementation/
│
├── docker-compose.yml       # Local dev environment
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
```

---

## 🔑 Environment Variables

### Backend (.env)
```env
NODE_ENV=development
PORT=3001

# Database
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://dev_user:dev_password@localhost:5432/task_automation_hub

# Redis
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# JWT
JWT_SECRET=your-super-secret-key-change-in-production
JWT_EXPIRY=24h
REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRY=7d

# Email (SMTP)
SMTP_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
SMTP_PORT=587
SMTP_USER=your-email@gmail.com
SMTP_PASSWORD=your-app-password
```

### Frontend (.env)
```env
VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3001/api/v1
VITE_WS_URL=http://localhost:3001
```

---

## 📚 API Endpoints (Summary)

### Auth
```
POST   /api/v1/auth/register
POST   /api/v1/auth/login
POST   /api/v1/auth/logout
POST   /api/v1/auth/refresh
GET    /api/v1/auth/me
```

### Tasks
```
GET    /api/v1/tasks              # List with pagination
POST   /api/v1/tasks              # Create
GET    /api/v1/tasks/:id          # Detail
PATCH  /api/v1/tasks/:id          # Update
DELETE /api/v1/tasks/:id          # Delete
POST   /api/v1/tasks/:id/assign   # Assign to user
```

### Notifications
```
GET    /api/v1/notifications
PATCH  /api/v1/notifications/:id/read
DELETE /api/v1/notifications/:id
```

---

## 🧪 Testing

```bash
# Backend tests
cd backend
npm test                    # Run all tests
npm run test:watch         # Watch mode
npm run test:coverage      # Coverage report

# Frontend tests
cd frontend
npm test                    # Run all tests
npm run test:ui            # Test UI dashboard
npm run test:coverage      # Coverage report
```

---

## 🔒 Security Checklist

- [x] HTTPS/TLS enforced (production)
- [x] JWT token management (24h expiry)
- [x] Refresh token rotation (HTTP-only cookie)
- [x] Password hashing (bcrypt, 12 rounds)
- [x] Input validation (Zod schemas)
- [x] CORS properly configured
- [x] Rate limiting on all endpoints
- [x] SQL injection prevention (Prisma ORM)
- [x] XSS prevention (Content-Type headers)
- [x] CSRF protection (SameSite cookies)
- [x] Audit logging

---

## 📊 Performance Targets (MVP)

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Frontend FCP | < 1.5s |
| API Response (p95) | < 200ms |
| Dashboard Load | < 2s |
| Concurrent Users | 1000+ |
| Uptime SLA | 99.5% |

---

## 📖 Documentation

See `/docs` for complete documentation:
- `phase-1-analysis/` - Requirements & opportunities
- `phase-2-design/ARCHITECTURE.md` - Full system design
- `phase-3-implementation/` - Implementation guides

---

## 🚀 Deployment

See deployment guides in `/docs/phase-3-implementation/` for:
- AWS ECS setup
- RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ
- ElastiCache Redis
- CloudFront CDN
- Monitoring & Logging

---

## 💬 Support

For issues and questions, check:
1. Architecture documentation in `/docs/phase-2-design/ARCHITECTURE.md`
2. Implementation guides in `/docs/phase-3-implementation/`
3. Code comments and type definitions

---

## 📅 Timeline

- **Week 1-2:** Project setup + Auth service
- **Week 2-3:** Task CRUD + Dashboard
- **Week 3-4:** Testing + Deployment

---

**Não precisa Visual Studio** - estou usando:

- ✅ **npm** (gerenciador de pacotes)

npm install       # Instala dependências

### ✅ OPÇÃO MELHOR PARA WINDOWS (sem Docker):
